steps.title: Infinitely Many Solutions Means the Equations Are Proportional
steps.content: When a system has infinitely many solutions, the two equations represent the same line. The constants show the scale factor from the first equation to the second: $$\frac{21/4}{3/5}=\frac{35}{4}$$ So every coefficient in the second equation must be $\frac{35}{4}$ times the matching coefficient in the first equation.

steps.title: Match the coefficients
steps.content: Multiplying the first equation's coefficients by $\frac{35}{4}$ gives $$g=\frac{35}{4}\cdot\frac{3}{7}=\frac{15}{4}$$ and $$k=\frac{35}{4}\cdot\frac{11}{7}=\frac{55}{4}$$ Therefore $$\frac{g}{k}=\frac{15/4}{55/4}=\frac{3}{11}$$ The exact answer is 3/11.
